description: Agentic routing data for the Coding Quality Loop. Map each profile to a real model on your platform. Models are placeholders; replace with your provider's identifiers. This is routing DATA consumed by hosts (Claude subagents, Droid droids, Codex subagents, Pi providers) — it is not a runtime. Per-role model routing is the host's job via the config profiles and the harness-agnostic role pack in assets/prompts/ and .claude/agents/. orchestrator and security_reviewer are mission/boundary roles, not per-step profiles.

orchestrator.role: Mission: scope, classify, context, spec/validation contract, decompose, assign workers, collect findings, stop if unsafe

repo_mapper.role: EXPLORE/CONTEXT MAP: smallest relevant code map; findings not dumps

minimality_reviewer.role: COMPLEXITY BRAKE: pick highest valid rung; simplicity review

fresh_reviewer.role: INDEPENDENT REVIEW: validator; diff review vs validation contract

security_reviewer.role: Risk boundaries only: auth, secrets, payments, PII, migrations, upload/download, network, shell, dependencies

packager.role: SHIP/HANDOFF: assemble PR handoff + completion record

retrospector.role: RETROSPECT: convert a repeated failure into a durable harness update (rule/test/hook/checklist)

low.note: One agent runs the whole loop.

high.note: Full split with enforced security/migration blocks; security_reviewer required at risk boundaries.

advisor.description: Anthropic advisor-tool pattern (https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/advisor-tool). A cheap executor drives the loop and consults a stronger reasoning model at reasoning walls only. The advisor never calls tools; it returns reasoning the executor acts on. DEFAULT for small/medium tasks -- cheaper than routing every step to a strong model, and it keeps one driver in control. Prefer this to a full multi-agent split until the task is genuinely high-risk.

memory.note: recall_budget_chars is the default char budget memory-recall uses when --budget is not passed explicitly; the CLI flag always wins.

control_plane.description: Local observability: control-index builds a SQLite index of this repo's agent sessions (model calls, exact token usage, tool calls) plus CQL artifacts (records, reviews, decisions, memory); control-serve renders it at http://127.0.0.1:<port>/. Opt-in and local-only: with enabled false the hooks write nothing and nothing is served. Set enabled true to let SessionStart hooks record events and (with autostart) launch the dashboard server. prices is YOUR price table (USD per million tokens, substring-matched against model ids) -- costs render only if you fill it; the skill ships no vendor price data.

model_routing.description: Per-model-class routing applied by setup-models. Copy this file to quality-loop.config.json at your repo root, set host to your platform, adjust your host's block, then run: python3 scripts/quality_loop.py setup-models. Hosts that use frontmatter (claude-code, droid) get their agent .md files rewritten; codex and pi print the settings to apply (PRINT-ONLY: never applied or verified by CQL). model_class values (cheap_fast, strong_reasoning, code_specialized) match the steps[].model_class field above. Capability annotations: cheap_fast = map, package, summarize, verify-orchestration; strong_reasoning = plan, review, debug, risk-assess; code_specialized = implement, test. Multi-host topologies: an agents entry may be an object {host, class} to pin that role to another harness, and main_session {host, class, model} declares where the implementer runs -- see assets/routing/ for three pre-validated variants (max-intelligence / balanced / max-throughput). check-config enforces reviewer heterogeneity across hosts on the model FAMILY (explicit family field or well-known prefix; unknown skips LOUDLY -- brief and check-config report SKIPPED naming the id): implementer and fresh_reviewer must not resolve to the same model, nor the same family unless allow_same_family:true, on medium+ tasks. Teach families for new/BYOK model ids via families ({model-id-or-prefix: family}, merged over the built-in table) so the check stays alive without a release. Effort ceiling: route at 'high' -- reasoning effort is per-step, not per-task endurance, so xhigh/max only overthink and overspend each step; check-config rejects xhigh/max unless a block sets allow_overthink:true for a genuinely ambiguous, architecture-sensitive case.

cheap_fast.note: Replace with a Factory model id (docs.factory.ai/models) or custom:<id> for BYOK; set family for BYOK ids the prefix match cannot classify.
Fill with your provider's model id.
